Dr Vandana Nathan

MBBS  ·  MD (Anatomy)

I like teaching anatomy like a story.
So here's my story.

Melbourne, Australia

↓ scroll

01
After Internship

I worked at Scudder Memorial Hospital.

A mission hospital in rural Tamil Nadu. Writing notes at the nurses station, a busy ward around me. A young doctor, quietly wondering what comes next.

02
The Preparation

Textbooks, a lamp,
and a question taking shape.

Preparing for postgraduate entrance exams. Long hours, anatomy textbooks spread open — building towards something not yet fully named, but felt in every diagram I studied.

03
The Decision

A fork in the road.
CMC Vellore called.

Multiple paths — one unmistakable pull. I chose an MD in Anatomy at Christian Medical College Vellore. At that crossroads, everything changed.

04
Falling Deeper In

Dissection. Histology.
Embryology. Surgical anatomy.

I walked into an Anatomy MD at Christian Medical College Vellore — one of India's most respected medical institutions — and never looked back. I loved every minute of it.

05
Finding My Voice

Five years. Lecture theatres. Microscopes.

Teaching first year MBBS cohorts at Karpagam Faculty of Medical Sciences — young students encountering the human body for the very first time. The doctor in me never really switched off.

06
The Leap

And then life brought us to Australia.

A leap of faith. A land of opportunity. I joined Monash University's Centre for Human Anatomy Education. Over 1,000 students a year across campuses. Something shifted.

07
Where I Belong

Every student made me
a better teacher.

Teaching students from diverse cultures and languages — across campuses, across backgrounds — didn't just shape my approach to anatomy. It made me love teaching even more. I also published two first-authored papers in radiological anatomy.

08
Grateful

Grateful for every blessing that brought me here.

This is the journey so far. Excited for what's ahead.

Meet Me There

Want to know how
I built this? Let's talk.

If these images sparked your curiosity — come collaborate with me. I'll be sharing the thinking, the tools, and the technique behind every detail.
